mechanism: Binary classification of stimuli by a hypervigilant system that treats unfamiliar features as pathogenic, conducted via memory confusion protocol testing whether different item categories share psychological representation, with output measuring within-group versus between-group confusion errors in cued recall.

Bayesian inference applied to probability judgments: integrate prior odds (group base rates) with likelihood of observed behavior (diagnostic of target role) to compute posterior probability. Compare observed judgments against model posteriors derived from Bayes' theorem with participant-elicited priors and likelihoods.

Episodic memory stores multi-element events as integrated representations via pattern completion. Index holistic retrieval by contingency between retrieval successes: compute proportion of events in which two associations within the same event are jointly correct/incorrect, subtract independent baseline (product of marginal accuracies) to isolate dependency beyond chance.

Cross-modal perception via emotional congruence: extract acoustic and visual features (fundamental frequency, formants, intensity, spectral properties, shape curvature), develop acoustic regression models to predict arousal, test whether arousal levels mediate sound-shape matching in forced-choice task via generalized linear mixed-effects model with arousal category as predictor.

Spatial attention orienting via dual-task paradigm: present emotional or neutral peripheral cues (faces) and measure disengagement by target detection speed on valid (same location) and invalid (different location) trials. Compare reaction time effects between emotional and neutral cues as proxy for engagement/disengagement rate; factor out general arousal by subtracting neutral baseline.

Motor performance maximization via factorial combination of three mechanisms: augmented feedback (outcome knowledge), external focus of attention (efferent copy control), and monetary reward (extrinsic motivation). Measure jump height (kinematic) and muscular activity (electromyographic) across six conditions via one-way repeated measures ANOVA.
